case_title: IN RE: 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NON

docket_entries.description: ORDER PER CC 4275(B) RE SERVICE OF PETITION AND SETTING HEARING DATE FOR DETERMINATION (HEARING:12/03/14; OPPOSITION:11/21/14)

PROOF OF SERVICE OF PETITION FOR COURT ORDER APPROVING AMENDMENT TO CC&RS; ORDER RE SERVICE OF PETITION TO AMEND CC&CRS FILED BY PETITIONER 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA NON PROFIT MUTUAL BENEFIT CORPORATION

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S VERIFIED PETITION FOR COURT ORDER APPROVING AMENDMENT TO CC&R'S (CC 4275 AND CORP. CODE 7515) AND/OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE TO REDUCE NUMBER OF VOTES AND PERCENTAGE FOR APPROVAL OF AMENDMENTS IS DENIED. ALTHOUGH THE REQUIREMENTS SET FORTH IN CIV. CODE SEC. 4275 ARE MET, PETITIONER HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT "BECAUSE OF VOTER APATHY OR OTHER REASONS, IMPORTANT AMENDMENTS CANNOT BE APPROVED BY THE NORMAL PROCEDURES'" (FOURTH LA COSTA CONDOMINIUM OWNERS ASS'N V. SEITH (2008) 159 CAL.APP.4TH 563, 570.) THE EVIDENCE ESTABLISHES THAT AN AMENDMENT WAS APPROVED BY A SUPER-MAJORITY OF VOTERS, AS REQUIRED BY THE CC&RS, IN THE VERY SAME ELECTION. (DECLARATION OF JUDITH TAYLOR, EX. 7.) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: SHERYL L. SAWYER, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-03-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, PETITIONER 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S PETITION FOR COURT ORDER APPROVING AMENDMENT TO CC&R'S AND/OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, TO REDUCE NUMBER OF VOTES AND PERCENTAGE FOR APPROVAL OF AMENDMENTS IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 15, 2014 TO GIVE MOVING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. MOVING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 3, 2014 AT 4:00 P.M. OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE PROOF OF SERVICE. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED. (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Continued Hearing On The Petition To Amend The Cc&R'S
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Notice Of Continued Hearing On The Petition To Amend The CC&R'S is off calendar. Duplicate entry. (302/EHG)

Petitioner 255 Berry Homeowners Association'S Petition For Court Order Approving Amendment To Cc&R'S And/Or In The Alternative To Reduce Number Of Votes And Percentage For Approval Of Amendments
Off Calendar Dec-15-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Atty. DAVID SWEDELSON of Swedelson & Gottlieb, Tel. No. (310) 207-2207 appearing for the Petitioner;
No other appearances made on this matter.

RULING - Petitioner's counsel did not give notice to the Court or the opposing party after the tentative ruling was issued. The Court gave Petitioner's counsel the chance to contact the opposing party this morning to argue the matter. The Court determined that the opposing party was given the opportunity to appear at today's hearing, and declined to do so. The matter was argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: PETITIONER 255 BERRY HOMEOWNERS ASSOCIATION'S Verified Petition For Court Order Approving Amendment To CC&R'S (CC 4275 and Corp. Code 7515) And/Or In The Alternative To Reduce Number Of Votes And Percentage For Approval Of Amendments is DENIED. Although the requirements set forth in Civ. Code sec. 4275 are met, Petitioner has not demonstrated that "because of voter apathy or other reasons, important amendments cannot be approved by the normal procedures'" (Fourth La Costa Condominium Owners Ass'n v. Seith (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 563, 570.) The evidence establishes that an amendment was approved by a super-majority of voters, as required by the CC&Rs, in the very same election. (Declaration of Judith Taylor, Ex. 7.) ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/EHG)

Petitioner 255 Berry Homeowners Association'S Petition For Court Order Approving Amendment To Cc&R'S And/Or In The Alternative To Reduce Number Of Votes And Percentage For Approval Of Amendments
Off Calendar Dec-03-2014 Continued To Dec-15-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Petitioner 255 Berry Homeowners Association's Petition For Court Order Approving Amendment To CC&R'S And/Or, In The Alternative, To Reduce Number Of Votes And Percentage For Approval Of Amendments is continued to December 15, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than December 3, 2014 at 4:00 p.m. or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the Proof of Service. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =302/ehg
